title: Sincere apology + immediate action
text: When the customer is angry at the restaurant directly, a heartfelt apology paired with a concrete fix works best.
options: Offer replacement order at no charge

title: Complimentary item or discount
text: Offer a free dessert, discount on next order, or loyalty points as a goodwill gesture after a serious complaint.

title: Manager callback
text: High-intensity anger should be escalated to a human manager who can call back within 15 minutes.

title: Empathetic apology for disappointment
text: The customer expected a good experience and felt let down. Acknowledge that disappointment gently.

title: Make it right — replacement or refund
text: For food quality or delivery failures that caused sadness, offer a direct replacement or refund.

title: Food safety reassurance
text: Customer worried about allergies, food safety, or wrong ingredients. Provide clear, calm information immediately.

title: Allergen information
text: Provide detailed allergen breakdown for any menu item. Reassure the customer we take food safety seriously.

title: Thank and invite back
text: Customer is happy with the experience. Mirror the joy, thank them, and invite them back or suggest their next visit.
options: Book a table for next visit

title: Loyalty reward for happy customers
text: Delighted customers are the best time to introduce loyalty rewards or upcoming events.

title: Address quality complaint directly
text: Customer found something wrong with the food — off smell, wrong texture, foreign object. Take it seriously immediately.

title: Escalate food quality issue
text: Foreign objects or spoiled food must be escalated to management and food safety team immediately.

title: Explain unexpected change
text: Customer surprised by a menu change, price change, or new policy. Acknowledge and explain clearly.

title: Order delay acknowledgement
text: Order is significantly late and the customer is frustrated. Acknowledge the delay, give real ETA, offer compensation.

title: Wrong order delivered
text: Customer received the wrong items. Acknowledge the error, confirm their actual order, arrange immediate correction.

title: Refund request handling
text: Customer requesting a refund due to a bad experience. Process quickly, no pushback on legitimate complaints.

title: Special occasion gone wrong
text: Customer was celebrating something and the experience fell short. Acknowledge the occasion, make amends.

title: Favourite item unavailable
text: Customer's favourite dish is sold out or no longer on the menu. Suggest similar alternatives warmly.

title: Calm food safety concern
text: Customer concerned about food handling, hygiene, or safety standards. Provide factual reassurance.

title: Happy with meal or service
text: Customer had a great experience overall. Amplify the joy, invite a review, and offer a reason to return.

title: Environment or packaging complaint
text: Customer disgusted by packaging, restaurant environment, or delivery conditions. Acknowledge and fix.

title: Surprised by new menu item or offer
text: Customer pleasantly or unpleasantly surprised by something new. Explain and guide them to next step.

title: Complaint about delivery partner
text: Customer angry at a delivery rider or third-party service. Acknowledge, take side of customer, escalate appropriately.

title: Ordering for someone who is unwell
text: Customer ordering for a sick or upset family member. Be especially warm and suggest suitable comfort options.

title: Ordering for a celebration or group
text: Customer ordering for a party or group celebration. Help them choose a great spread and possibly a cake.

title: Ordering for someone with dietary restrictions
text: Customer worried about ordering correctly for someone with allergies or dietary requirements.

title: Comfort food suggestion
text: Customer seems down and ordering for themselves. Suggest warm, comforting dishes that feel like a treat.

title: Treating yourself — celebrate with a good meal
text: Customer is happy and treating themselves. Encourage with premium options, chef's specials, or a dessert pairing.

title: Stress-eating comfort food
text: Customer frustrated with themselves or a bad day. Gently suggest comfort food that genuinely helps them feel better.

title: Frustrated while waiting for order update
text: Customer is angry because they can't see where their order is. Validate the frustration first, then give them a live status update with estimated arrival.

title: Anxiously waiting and feeling low
text: Customer is sad or worried about where their order is — perhaps it's for an important occasion. Offer warmth and a clear update.
options: Your order is on its way

title: Worried order may be lost or wrong
text: Customer is anxious their order was lost, sent to the wrong address, or mixed up. Calmly confirm the order details and current status.

title: Order status changed unexpectedly
text: Customer surprised to see order status change (e.g., went back to 'preparing' or shows 'delivered' when they haven't received it). Explain the status clearly.

title: Excited and eagerly waiting for order
text: Customer is happy and excitedly checking on their order. Share the excitement and give them a precise ETA to build anticipation.

title: Furious about significantly late order
text: Customer is very angry their order is way past the promised time. Acknowledge the anger directly, give a real ETA, and offer immediate compensation — no excuses first.

title: Disappointed and let down by late order
text: Customer feels let down — maybe the food was for a special moment or they were very hungry. Empathise with the disappointment and reassure them.

title: Worried the order is lost due to delay
text: Customer is anxious the long delay means the order is lost or the rider had an accident. Calmly confirm the order is being tracked and safe.

title: Disgusted by repeated delays or cold food risk
text: Customer is disgusted — either this is a repeated delay issue or they fear the food will arrive cold and inedible. Address both the emotional state and the food quality concern.
options: Full refund if food arrives cold

title: Shocked that order is this late
text: Customer didn't expect such a long wait and is surprised. Acknowledge that this is unusual, explain the cause briefly, and give a firm updated ETA.

title: Outraged at receiving wrong item
text: Customer is furious that the wrong food arrived, especially if they're hungry or it's an allergy risk. Own the mistake completely, no deflection, and offer the fastest possible fix.

title: Sad and disappointed about wrong item
text: Customer was really looking forward to a specific dish and got the wrong one. Acknowledge how disappointing that feels and make it right warmly.

title: Scared wrong item has allergens
text: Customer received the wrong item and is now afraid they may have consumed something they're allergic to. This is urgent — reassure and provide ingredient info immediately.
options: Connect to kitchen for allergen info

title: Disgusted by wrong item — especially if non-veg sent to veg customer
text: Customer is disgusted — the wrong item violates their personal, cultural, or dietary values. Treat this with maximum sensitivity and urgency.

title: Confused and surprised by wrong item
text: Customer is more puzzled than angry — they just got something completely unexpected. Acknowledge the confusion, confirm their original order, and correct it.

title: Angry customer demanding refund
text: Customer is angry and demanding a refund for a genuine failure. Do not challenge or delay. Acknowledge the anger, validate their right to a refund, and process it swiftly.
options: Offer store credit as faster alternative

title: Sad and resigned customer asking for refund
text: Customer is not shouting but is quietly disappointed and just wants their money back. Meet that quiet sadness with genuine warmth and process their refund without friction.
options: Offer to make it right instead

title: Disgusted and seeking refund for quality failure
text: Customer wants a refund because the food quality was unacceptable — spoiled, contaminated, or deeply unpleasant. Treat with maximum urgency and do not question their experience.

title: Anxious about whether refund will come through
text: Customer is worried their refund may not be processed correctly or may take too long. Provide clear, firm assurance about the timeline and method.
options: Escalate if not received in 5 days

title: Surprised their refund was denied or partially approved
text: Customer expected a full refund but got something different. Acknowledge their surprise, explain the policy clearly, and escalate if it was a genuine error.
